qa_pairs.answer: To apply to Xiamen University Malaysia (XMUM), you can choose one of three methods: online, email, or paper submission. First, select your preferred method and prepare required documents such as academic transcripts, identification documents, and proof of payment for application fees. Then submit your application through the chosen method: online via the portal https://apply.xmu.edu.my/, email to admissions@xmu.edu.my, or by submitting a printed form to the Admissions Office. After submission, pay the RM300 application and registration fee (additional SST applies for international students if applicable). Your application will then be reviewed by the Admissions Office, and you may be contacted for additional documents. Required documents include transcripts, certificates, NRIC or passport, and proof of payment.

qa_pairs.answer:
Xiamen University Malaysia (XMUM) offers several computing and technology-related degree programmes under the School of Computing and Data Science, including Software Engineering, Computer Science and Technology, Data Science, Cyber Security, and Digital Media Technology. All programmes are full-time undergraduate degrees with a duration of 4 years and are taught in English.

Applicants are typically required to meet academic entry requirements such as STPM (Science Stream: Grade C in Mathematics and 1 Science/ICT subject; Non-Science Stream: Grade C in 2 subjects plus credit in Additional Mathematics), A-Level (minimum Grade D in 2 subjects), UEC (at least Grade B in 5 subjects), Foundation/Matriculation (minimum CGPA 2.0), or a relevant Diploma in computing or related fields (with CGPA requirements depending on qualification).

The programmes are designed with a strong balance of theory and practical learning, covering areas such as programming, system design, data structures, and cybersecurity. Students also gain exposure to industry-relevant tools, real-world projects, and internship opportunities, helping them develop practical skills and improve employability in the technology sector.

XMUM scholarship GPA requirement
XMUM minimum CGPA scholarship
What results do I need to keep my XMUM scholarship?
XMUM scholarship maintain grades CGPA
qa_pairs.answer:
To maintain an XMUM scholarship, students must meet CGPA and performance requirements based on their scholarship type.

For study grants:
- Teacher’s Children Grant: minimum CGPA 2.00
- Sibling Grant: minimum CGPA 2.00
- Bumiputera & Indian Community (BIC) Grant: minimum CGPA 2.00

For Merit Scholarships, renewal is based on academic achievement and ranking among peers in the same programme and same intake.

Scholarship renewal depends on the student’s academic achievement at the end of each academic year compared to their peers from the same programme and same intake.

Scholarship renewal criteria:
- 80% < scholarship ≤ 100%: Top 25%
- 50% < scholarship ≤ 80%: Top 35%
- 25% < scholarship ≤ 50%: Top 45%
- Scholarship ≤ 25%: Top 70%

If the required CGPA or ranking is not met, the scholarship may be reduced or discontinued for the following academic year.

For official details, refer to: https://www.xmu.edu.my/admissions/scholarships-financial-aid
